source_paper_title: The Complexity of Recognizing Facets for the Knapsack Polytope
context:
Page 16 (PDF page 16/17), Section 5 "Concluding Remarks"

It is still open whether it can be done in time f(K)· poly(n) where f is some computable function independent of n, i.e., whether the knapsack facet recognition problem is fixed-parameter tractable.
title: Fixed-parameter tractability of knapsack facet recognition parameterized by the number of distinct coefficients
background_latex:
Let 
- 
\[Q = \{x\in\{0,1\}^n : a^\top x \le b\}\]
be a (0-1) knapsack set with \((a,b)\in \mathbb{N}^{n+1}\) and knapsack polytope \(P=\mathrm{conv}(Q)\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\).
- \(\alpha\in\mathbb{Z}_+^n\) and \(\beta\in\mathbb{Z}_+\) define a linear inequality \(\alpha^\top x \le \beta\).
- The inequality \(\alpha^\top x\le \beta\) is facet-defining for \(P\) if it is valid for \(P\) and the face \(\{x\in P: \alpha^\top x = \beta\}\) has dimension \(\dim(P)-1\).
- Let \(K := |\alpha|_+\) denote the number of distinct positive coefficients in \(\alpha\), i.e.,
\[K = \left|\{\alpha_i : \alpha_i>0,\ i\in[n]\}\right|.\]
- Consider the decision problem \(\textsc{KnapsackFacets}\): given \((a,b,\alpha,\beta)\), decide whether \(\alpha^\top x\le\beta\) defines a facet of \(P\).
- A parameterized algorithm is fixed-parameter tractable (FPT) in parameter \(K\) if it runs in time \(f(K)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(n)\) for some computable function \(f\) independent of \(n\).
problem_definition_latex: Determine whether \(\textsc{KnapsackFacets}\) parameterized by \(K=|\alpha|_+\) is fixed-parameter tractable, i.e., whether there exists a computable function \(f\) and a polynomial \(p\) such that for every instance \((a,b,\alpha,\beta)\) the question “does \(\alpha^\top x\le\beta\) define a facet of \(P=\mathrm{conv}(\{x\in\{0,1\}^n: a^\top x\le b\})\)?” can be decided in time \(f(K)\cdot p(n)\).
latex:
Let 
- 
\[Q = \{x\in\{0,1\}^n : a^\top x \le b\}\]
be a (0-1) knapsack set with \((a,b)\in \mathbb{N}^{n+1}\) and knapsack polytope \(P=\mathrm{conv}(Q)\subseteq\mathbb{R}^n\).
- \(\alpha\in\mathbb{Z}_+^n\) and \(\beta\in\mathbb{Z}_+\) define a linear inequality \(\alpha^\top x \le \beta\).
- The inequality \(\alpha^\top x\le \beta\) is facet-defining for \(P\) if it is valid for \(P\) and the face \(\{x\in P: \alpha^\top x = \beta\}\) has dimension \(\dim(P)-1\).
- Let \(K := |\alpha|_+\) denote the number of distinct positive coefficients in \(\alpha\), i.e.,
\[K = \left|\{\alpha_i : \alpha_i>0,\ i\in[n]\}\right|.\]
- Consider the decision problem \(\textsc{KnapsackFacets}\): given \((a,b,\alpha,\beta)\), decide whether \(\alpha^\top x\le\beta\) defines a facet of \(P\).
- A parameterized algorithm is fixed-parameter tractable (FPT) in parameter \(K\) if it runs in time \(f(K)\cdot \mathrm{poly}(n)\) for some computable function \(f\) independent of \(n\).

Determine whether \(\textsc{KnapsackFacets}\) parameterized by \(K=|\alpha|_+\) is fixed-parameter tractable, i.e., whether there exists a computable function \(f\) and a polynomial \(p\) such that for every instance \((a,b,\alpha,\beta)\) the question “does \(\alpha^\top x\le\beta\) define a facet of \(P=\mathrm{conv}(\{x\in\{0,1\}^n: a^\top x\le b\})\)?” can be decided in time \(f(K)\cdot p(n)\).
open_problem_quote: It is still open whether it can be done in time f(K)· poly(n) where f is some computable function independent of n, i.e., whether the knapsack facet recognition problem is fixed-parameter tractable.

verification.notice: This entry was generated from the paper text only. It has not been independently verified as still open. If you believe the question is solved or incorrectly stated, please contact the maintainers.
